rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended And Supplemental Petition And Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Trust, Aiding And Abetting Breach Of Trust, And Fraud
rulings.Rulings: Continued to March 18, 2015 for delivery of courtesy copies of the amended and supplemented petition and amendment to the petition (filed 2/5/15), plus courtesy copies of any objections to those pleadings. Please do not provide any papers related to law and motion matters. Copies must be delivered to this Examiner in room 202 no later than Friday, March 13, 2015. In addition, notice of the amendment was not proper as there is no proof of service of the Notice of Hearing form, mandatory JC form DE-120. Waivers of notice must be filed by 3/13/15 or this matter will have to be continued further to allow proper notice.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHARLES YOUNG CHONG et al TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings And Request For Attorney Fees And Costs To Be Charged Against Petitioners Share (By Trustee Aaron B. Chong)
rulings.Rulings: The motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend. The request for attorney's fees and costs is denied. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the court's tentative ruling shall notify court and opposing counsel no later than 3/3/2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE INNOCENTE CASSOLATO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Carol Cassolatos Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify Subpoena Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order For Consumer Financial Records Requested From Non-Party Costanzo Buzzelli By Stephen Cassolato, Former Trustee; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; And Declaration Of Kathryn A. Schofield In Support
rulings.Rulings: The Court grants the motion to quash or modify the subpoena. The request for the entirety of Carol's personal financial records is overbroad. The parties shall meet and confer no later than March 20, 2015 to narrow the scope of the subpoena so that Carol's privacy rights will not be violated. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative shall notify the court and opposing counsel no later than 3/3/15, at 4:00 p.m.
